content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
File 
Date 
Jan. 11, 1977 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Ted Prim 
Subject: 
Letter from Edith Brewer. 
I told her that currently there were no procedures for 
terminating a committee. 
However, if one were not re-
ceiving contributions or making expenditures one need not 
file reports. 
Thus, by terminating all activities one can 
eliminate virtually all obligations under the Political Re-
form Act. 
I also told her that we were contemplating a 
regulation which would establish a termination procedure. 
She actually seemed to be more interested in how a person 
terminated his relationship with a campaign committee. 
I 
indicated that pursuant to our Act, no procedure was neces-
sary except that with respect to treasurers and principal 
officers, the Statement of Organization would have to be 
amended by the committee. 
I told her that if she or the person in question wished to 
discuss this matter further, I would be happy to do so.

Verne i.L Perl'yman 
967 West G Street 
Ontario, California 91762

Thank you for your letter dated April 13. 1976 which IHlvi!>ed our office that your 
committee, Committee to Elec( Anthony "Tony" Rizzo, ID# 746680 has become 
inactive. 
The delay in responding to your request was due to tl!.e fact that the Political Reform 
Act contains no provisions by which a committee may terminate; nor has the fair 
-Politicai PractIces Commission Issued any f'egulations In this area. Your statement 
of organization, together with youl' letter, will be placed in an inactive file. If 
your committee receives contributions or makes expenditures, please immediately 
notify our office that your committee has again become active. 
Please be aware that regulations or legislation mCiY corne into effect which would 
influence your committee status. Further questions pertaining to the status of your 
committee should be directed to the Technical Assistance .and Analysis Division 
of the Fair Political Practices Commission at (916) 322-5660. 
If our office may be of any assistance to you, do not hesitate to contact the under-
signed. 
Sincerely, 
WILLIAM N. DURLEY 
Assistant to the Secretary of State 
Elections and Political Reform

sections.conclusion: Your statement of organization, together with your letter, will be placed in an inactive file. If your committee receives contributions or makes expenditures, please immediately notify our office that your committee has again become active. Please be aware that regulations or legislation may come into effect which would influence your committee status.
sections.question_synthetic: How can a campaign committee be terminated or discontinued, and what procedures and filing obligations apply when a committee becomes inactive?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC placed the inactive committee in an inactive file with no formal termination procedure currently available; however, committees that cease all activities and don't receive contributions or make expenditures have no reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple related items: internal FPPC memorandum from Ted Prim, formal FPPC response letter to Verne Perryman from William N. Durley, and transmittal letter from State Senator Ruben Ayala's office. Core advice letter is the formal response dated October 20, 1976. Some text quality issues with OCR artifacts in the Senator's letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How can a campaign committee be terminated or discontinued, and what procedures and filing obligations apply when a committee becomes inactive?

CONCLUSION: Your statement of organization, together with your letter, will be placed in an inactive file. If your committee receives contributions or makes expenditures, please immediately notify our office that your committee has again become active. Please be aware that regulations or legislation may come into effect which would influence your committee status.

I told her that currently there were no procedures for terminating a committee. However, if one were not re- ceiving contributions or making expenditures one need not file reports. Thus, by terminating all activities one can eliminate virtually all obligations under the Political Re- form Act. She actually seemed to be more interested in how a person terminated his relationship with a campaign committee. I told her that if she or the person in question wished to discuss this matter further, I would be happy to do so. embedding.summary: This advice letter from the FPPC responds to inquiries about committee termination procedures, clarifying that no formal termination provisions exist in the Political Reform Act but that inactive committees with no financial activity have minimal reporting obligations.
